verdict.summary: No skill and Apollo each passed 3 of 3 tasks. rust-craft passed 2 of 3 and failed one critical FFI assertion.

task_findings.finding: All configurations restored the MSRV API, feature edge, downstream trait compatibility, and package contents.
All configurations removed the in-flight record before await and requeued it only on an observed sink error.
rust-craft contained the panic but prioritized a simultaneous foreign status, returning ForeignStatus(7) instead of the required CallbackPanicked. No skill and Apollo prioritized CallbackPanicked.

strengths: Exact skill, task, and fixture snapshots were frozen and verified before execution.
Each executor was fresh, isolated, and received only its assigned repository plus the selected skill snapshot.
All final native gates were independently rerun before grading.
Graders were blind to configuration and recorded evidence per predeclared assertion.
The FFI grader used independent panic-plus-status and Send/Sync probes.
limitations: One run per task and configuration is not a reliability estimate.
The harness exposes global skills, so without_skill relies on an explicit prohibition rather than physical removal.
Effective executor and grader model identities, traces, and token counts were unavailable.
The isolated default toolchain was Rust 1.96; the async fixture's declared Rust 1.85 MSRV was not executed.
The preflight reference solution passed native checks but would fail the grader's combined callback-panic plus foreign-status probe; candidates A and B nevertheless provide verified passing paths.
Release and async remained saturated across all three configurations.

disposition.skill_effect: No positive effect observed; rust-craft underperformed both controls on one task in this single pilot.
disposition.skill_change: Do not change rust-craft from one trial alone. First decide and encode the intended precedence between callback panic and simultaneous foreign failure, then repeat the discriminating task.
